---
title: "10 - Example Network Visualization with igraph"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    number_sections: true
---

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(igraph)
})
```

# Goal

Plot example GRNs from the igraph objects already generated in 10_network_analysis_igraph.Rmd.

# Load saved networks

```{r load-saved-networks}
list_grn <- readRDS(
  file.path(
    "results",
    "bcell_grn_by_cell_type.rds"
  )
)

load(
  file.path(
    "results",
    "bcell_grn_global.RData"
  )
)

names(list_grn)
net
```

# Helper plotting function

```{r plot-helper}
plot_grn_example <- function(
  graph,
  title = "Example GRN",
  top_n = 80,
  seed = 123
) {
  set.seed(seed)

  if (igraph::vcount(graph) == 0) {
    message("Empty graph")
    return(NULL)
  }

  node_degree <- igraph::degree(graph)
  node_degree <- as.numeric(node_degree)
  names(node_degree) <- igraph::V(graph)$name

  top_nodes <- names(
    sort(
      node_degree,
      decreasing = TRUE
    )[seq_len(min(top_n, length(node_degree)))]
  )

  graph_sub <- igraph::induced_subgraph(
    graph,
    vids = top_nodes
  )

  sub_degree <- igraph::degree(graph_sub)
  sub_degree <- as.numeric(sub_degree)
  names(sub_degree) <- igraph::V(graph_sub)$name

  plot(
    graph_sub,
    layout = igraph::layout_with_fr(graph_sub),
    vertex.size = log(sub_degree + 2) * 4,
    vertex.label.cex = 0.6,
    vertex.label.color = "black",
    vertex.color = "lightblue",
    vertex.frame.color = "grey40",
    edge.arrow.size = 0.25,
    edge.color = "grey70",
    main = title
  )
}
```

# Plot global GRN example

```{r plot-global-grn, fig.width=10, fig.height=10}
pdf(file.path("results","example_global_grn_plot.pdf"), width = 10, height = 10)
plot_grn_example(
  graph = net,
  title = "Global B-cell GRN",
  top_n = 100
)
dev.off()
```

# Plot one cell-type-specific GRN

```{r plot-one-cell-type, fig.width=10, fig.height=10}
target_cell_type <- "Naive_CD5neg"
pdf(file.path("results",paste0("example_", target_cell_type, "_grn_plot.pdf")), width = 10, height = 10)

plot_grn_example(
  graph = list_grn[[target_cell_type]],
  title = paste(target_cell_type, "GRN"),
  top_n = 80
)
dev.off()
```

# Plot TF-centered network

```{r tf-centered-network, fig.width=8, fig.height=8}
# PAX5 is a well-known TF in B-cell development. Let's plot its ego network in the global GRN.
# ensembl ID: ENSG00000196092

target_tf <- "ENSG00000196092" # PAX5
target_tf_name <- "PAX5"
if (target_tf %in% V(net)$name) {

  tf_neighbors <- ego(
    net,
    order = 1,
    nodes = target_tf,
    mode = "out"
  )[[1]]

  tf_graph <- induced_subgraph(
    net,
    vids = tf_neighbors
  )
  pdf(file.path("results",paste0("example_", target_tf_name, "_grn_plot.pdf")), width = 10, height = 10)
  plot(
    tf_graph,
    layout = layout_with_fr(tf_graph),
    vertex.size = 8,
    vertex.label.cex = 0.75,
    vertex.label.color = "black",
    vertex.color = ifelse(V(tf_graph)$name == target_tf, "tomato", "lightblue"),
    vertex.frame.color = "grey40",
    edge.arrow.size = 0.3,
    edge.color = "grey70",
    main = paste(target_tf, "outgoing regulatory network")
  )
  dev.off()
} else {

  message(
    target_tf,
    " was not found in the global GRN. Try another TF name."
  )
}
```
